channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What a chimpanzee 'civil war' can teach us about how societies fall apart
item.description: Researchers have observed a "civil war" amongst wild chimpanzees for the first time.
item.content:encoded: Researchers have observed a "civil war" amongst wild chimpanzees for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Why Pokemon cards are one of the hottest speculative booms right now
item.description: If you collected Pokemon cards as a kid, here's hoping you held onto them. The Japanese franchise has been popular for decades, but it's become the latest speculative boom.
item.content:encoded: If you collected Pokemon cards as a kid, here's hoping you held onto them. The Japanese franchise has been popular for decades, but it's become the latest speculative boom.
item.dc:creator: Ricky Mulvey

item.title: Iranians weigh in on the fragile ceasefire
item.description: We head the the border between Iran and Turkey to hear what Iranians have to say about the fragile ceasefire between Iran, the U.S. and Israel.
item.content:encoded: We head the the border between Iran and Turkey to hear what Iranians have to say about the fragile ceasefire between Iran, the U.S. and Israel.
item.dc:creator: D. Parvaz

item.title: One miner has been rescued from a flooded tunnel in Mexico after 14 days
item.description: A dramatic rescue in Mexico - after 14 days trapped deep underground a miner is rescued alive from a flooded tunnel—while another remains missing below.
item.content:encoded: A dramatic rescue in Mexico - after 14 days trapped deep underground a miner is rescued alive from a flooded tunnel—while another remains missing below.
item.dc:creator: Katie Silver

item.title: Three months after Maduro's capture, there's an air of home in Venezuela
item.description: Three months after Nicolás Maduro's capture, Venezuelans are daring to hope again — even as the hardest part may still lie ahead.
item.content:encoded: Three months after Nicolás Maduro's capture, Venezuelans are daring to hope again — even as the hardest part may still lie ahead.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: U.S. fertility continues to drop
item.description: Americans are having fewer and fewer children. New numbers out today show the continuation of a trend that could change many things about life in the United States.
item.content:encoded: Americans are having fewer and fewer children. New numbers out today show the continuation of a trend that could change many things about life in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Debate over Kansas hamburger stand's mural could head to the Supreme Court
item.description: A dispute over a Kansas hamburger stand's mural could go all the way to the U.S. Supreme Court. The outcome could affect hundreds of municipalities across the country.
item.content:encoded: A dispute over a Kansas hamburger stand's mural could go all the way to the U.S. Supreme Court. The outcome could affect hundreds of municipalities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Algeo

item.title: A fragile ceasefire and emboldened Iran
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with International correspondent Daniel Estrin about how the US-Iran-Israel ceasefire is being received in Israel.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with International correspondent Daniel Estrin about how the US-Iran-Israel ceasefire is being received in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: How Trump's foreign policy is playing in North Carolina
item.description: What do people who follow foreign affairs make of the war with Iran and the president's foreign policy? NPR spoke with a dozen World Affairs Council members in North Carolina to find out.
item.content:encoded: What do people who follow foreign affairs make of the war with Iran and the president's foreign policy? NPR spoke with a dozen World Affairs Council members in North Carolina to find out.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: A new study helps explain how the brain creates mental images
item.description: A new study finds that looking at something and imagining it triggers the same exact process in the brain. It's also very similar to the process artificial intelligence uses to create an image.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds that looking at something and imagining it triggers the same exact process in the brain. It's also very similar to the process artificial intelligence uses to create an image.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton
